name: fd (Fast find Alternative)

performance.notes: Rust-based; 5-10x faster than find on large directories

agent_readiness.notes: User-friendly find replacement. `fd pattern`. Case-insensitive by default. Respects .gitignore. Colored output. -t f/d/l for file/directory/symlink. -e extension filter. --exec/-x for parallel execution. --exclude patterns. -H for hidden files. Regex support. --max-depth. Parallel directory traversal. From sharkdp (bat, hyperfine). Standard in developer dotfiles. `fd '.*\.py$' src/ --exec black {}`. Much friendlier syntax than find's -name '*.py' pattern.

gotchas: CLI tool only — not a library
best_when: Replacing find in developer scripts with readable syntax
Integration with fzf for interactive file selection
avoid_when: POSIX find compatibility required (shell scripts on minimal systems)
Non-interactive scripts where find is more universal
summary: fd is a fast, user-friendly find replacement. `fd pattern` — case-insensitive, .gitignore-aware, colored output. -t file/dir, -e extension, --exec for parallel execution. 5-10x faster than find. Rust-based from the bat/hyperfine author. Standard in developer dotfiles alongside bat, fzf, ripgrep. Much friendlier syntax than POSIX find for everyday file discovery.
